prose:
Method–provenance block  
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack) and the dual descriptor for benzaldehyde; headline value reported below is the largest f⁺ (most electrophilic site).  
• Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier ‘standard’); exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF).  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (benzaldehyde.xyz, 14 atoms); single-point Fukui evaluation on this fixed geometry (no re-optimization).  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  
• Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (singlet). N − 1 and N + 1 states computed as doublets (charge = ±1, mult = 2) for the finite-difference Fukui derivatives.  
• Software: PySCF 2023.x via chemkit fukui-reactivity skill.  
• Convergence & warnings: all SCF calculations converged; integrity status “ok”, trustworthy = true.  chemkit issued three warnings (reproduced verbatim above) noting charge conservation checks (sums of f⁺ and f⁻ exactly 1.0000) and presence of the schema header.  
• Live log: calculation ran with command  
    /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui benzaldehyde.xyz --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 …  
  and streamed to a live `.out` file (path was disclosed during execution and remains on disk).  
• Output JSON saved to: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde_fukui_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json  

Key results (per-atom Mulliken Fukui indices):  
  – Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C of the carbonyl carbon), f⁺ = 0.39938967  
  – Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (carbonyl O),             f⁻ = 0.23839906  
  – Dual descriptor highlights the same carbon (0.3626) as electrophilic and oxygen (−0.3446) as nucleophilic.  

Headline number (requested “result”): f⁺ on the most electrophilic atom = 0.39938967 (dimensionless).
